title: 💻 Copilot Code Impact [Edu] [v2]
description: Measures the volume and quality of Copilot-assisted code contributions including lines suggested, accepted, acceptance rate, and PR attribution.

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE mode
    WHEN 'live' THEN '📡 Live Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'seed' THEN '🌱 Synthetic Seed Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'demo' THEN '🎮 Demo Environment — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    ELSE '❓ Unknown Mode'
  END AS "Data Source"
FROM data_mode
ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1

options.content:
# 💻 Copilot Code Impact

**Supplementary Metrics — AI-Assisted Code Volume**

## What it Measures
This dashboard quantifies the volume and acceptance rate of code suggestions made by GitHub Copilot. It tracks lines suggested versus lines accepted across the 28-day window, and counts pull requests attributed to Copilot-active developers.

## Why it Matters
Acceptance rate and lines accepted are the primary signals of whether Copilot's suggestions are relevant and high-quality. Declining acceptance rates may indicate suggestion quality issues, mismatched context, or developers who have not yet learned to prompt effectively.

> ⚠️ **Attribution caveat**: A PR is counted as "Copilot-attributed" when the author has an active Copilot seat (activity within 28 days). This is a *proxy* — GitHub provides no per-PR Copilot telemetry. All Copilot attribution panels show this caveat.

## How to Interpret
| Metric | Healthy Signal | What it Means |
|---|---|---|
| **Acceptance Rate** | ≥30% | Suggestions are relevant and trusted by developers |
| **Lines Accepted** | Growing over time | Copilot is contributing meaningfully to code output |
| **Copilot-Attributed PRs** | High % of total PRs | Active users are shipping regularly |

panels.description: Copilot Code Impact — lines accepted, acceptance rate, and attributed PRs.

panels.title: Lines Accepted (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Accepted (28d)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` → `loc_added_sum` | Tables: `copilot_org_metrics`

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` → `loc_added_sum`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_org_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Sum of `loc_added_sum` across daily rows in the selected date range. 'Accepted' means kept at suggestion time — the developer may immediately delete accepted lines. Org-level aggregate only; no per-repo or per-team breakdown.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Accepted (28d)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.title: Lines Suggested (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum) AS "Lines Suggested (28d)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` → `loc_suggested_to_add_sum` | Tables: `copilot_org_metrics`

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` → `loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_org_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Total lines Copilot suggested (accepted or rejected) in the selected date range. Same download_links → NDJSON fetch pattern as all Copilot Metrics Reports endpoints. Org-level aggregate only — no per-repository or per-team breakdown.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum) AS "Lines Suggested (28d)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.title: Acceptance Rate (%)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1
) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` (API version 2026-03-10) → NDJSON → `loc_added_sum / loc_suggested_to_add_sum` | Tables: `copilot_org_metrics`

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` (API version 2026-03-10) → NDJSON → `loc_added_sum / loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_org_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Ratio of accepted lines to suggested lines. The endpoint returns a `download_links` envelope (not inline JSON) — the sync server must fetch the signed S3 URL separately and parse each line as a distinct JSON object (NDJSON format).

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT ROUND(
  SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1
) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.title: Copilot-Attributed PRs

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id) AS "Copilot-Attributed PRs"
FROM pull_requests pr
JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.description: Source: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` (proxy — no per-PR Copilot signal in GitHub API) | Tables: `pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` (proxy — no per-PR Copilot signal in GitHub API)

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Count of merged PRs where the PR author has an active Copilot seat. ⚠️ **Proxy metric** — GitHub provides no per-PR or per-commit signal indicating Copilot was actually used. A developer may have written this PR entirely without Copilot.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id) AS "Copilot-Attributed PRs"
FROM pull_requests pr
JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
```

panels.title: Lines Accepted vs Suggested over Time

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  day AS time,
  loc_suggested_to_add_sum AS "Lines Suggested",
  loc_added_sum AS "Lines Added"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` → daily rows | Tables: `copilot_org_metrics`

panels.title: Lines Accepted vs Suggested over Time - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` → daily rows

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_org_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Time series showing both `loc_suggested_to_add_sum` and `loc_added_sum` per day. Coverage is limited to the last 28 days (rolling window from today). The current calendar day may be incomplete if the sync ran before end of day.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  day AS time,
  loc_suggested_to_add_sum AS "Lines Suggested",
  loc_added_sum AS "Lines Added"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day
```

panels.title: Acceptance Rate Trend

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  day AS time,
  ROUND(loc_added_sum::numeric / NULLIF(loc_suggested_to_add_sum, 0) * 100, 1) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `totals_by_language` JSONB array → `loc_added_sum / loc_suggested_to_add_sum` | Tables: `copilot_org_metrics`

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API → `totals_by_language` JSONB array → `loc_added_sum / loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_org_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Daily acceptance rate computed via `jsonb_array_elements(totals_by_language)` unnesting. JSONB array expansion is more expensive than scalar column access. On low-activity days, a small number of suggestions can swing the rate from 0% to 100%.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  day AS time,
  ROUND(loc_added_sum::numeric / NULLIF(loc_suggested_to_add_sum, 0) * 100, 1) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day
```

panels.title: Copilot PR Rate (%)

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN pr.id END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id), 0), 1
) AS "Copilot PR Rate (%)"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.description: Source: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` (proxy — no per-PR Copilot signal exists in GitHub API) | Tables: `pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` (proxy — no per-PR Copilot signal exists in GitHub API)

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Percentage of merged PRs whose author has an active Copilot seat. ⚠️ **Proxy metric** — the GitHub API provides no per-PR or per-commit signal indicating Copilot was used. Active seat is the only available signal.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN pr.id END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id), 0), 1
) AS "Copilot PR Rate (%)"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
```

panels.title: Top 10 by Lines Added (Copilot Users)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  user_login AS "Developer",
  SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Added",
  ROUND(SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
GROUP BY user_login
ORDER BY "Lines Added" DESC
LIMIT 10

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` (2026-03-10) → per-user NDJSON | Tables: `copilot_user_metrics`

panels.title: Top 10 by Lines Added (Copilot Users) - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` (2026-03-10) → per-user NDJSON

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Top 10 users by `loc_added_sum` from the **per-user** 28-day report — a **separate endpoint** from the org report. Same download_links → NDJSON pattern. Per-user data is visible to org owners and billing managers.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  user_login AS "Developer",
  SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Added",
  ROUND(SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
GROUP BY user_login
ORDER BY "Lines Added" DESC
LIMIT 10
```

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Row count of `copilot_seats` table — a data health indicator. If 0, the last sync did not load seat data (check server logs for a silent 403). The table is fully replaced on every sync (TRUNCATE + INSERT), unlike PRs and deployments (incremental).

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats
```

panels.title: Last Successful Sync

targets.rawSql: SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'

panels.description: Source: Internal `sync_jobs` table (no GitHub API call) | Tables: `sync_jobs`

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source

Internal `sync_jobs` table (no GitHub API call)

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`sync_jobs`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

`MAX(finished_at)` from `sync_jobs` where `status='success'`. A 'success' status does NOT guarantee all resources were fetched — individual fetchers can fail silently (e.g., 403 on Copilot seats) while the overall job still completes with 'success'.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'
```
